title: 'This is the path I plan to pursue' — Apr 2019 essay-fragment / pitch-line
html:
TL;DR

  • One-sentence Apple Note from Apr 1, 2019 — "This is the path I plan to pursue, and with the experience I gain with you, I plan to take this path to new endeavors." Yahir is 16, junior year of high school.
  • Reads as either a scholarship-essay closer or an FBLA pitch line being workshopped — the "with the experience I gain with you" suggests it's addressed to a specific judge/program/sponsor, in second-person-formal mode. Not a manifesto, not a journal — a rehearsal-line.
  • Sits chronologically right between the Mar 2019 cognition question and the Mar 27 Forsaken for me mortality note. Spring 2019 is the applying-for-everything season — FBLA, Ford, JCEF, OSU applications all live in this window.
  • Earliest extant specimen of Yahir's path-language — the framing of life as an itinerary that compounds, where each program is a stepping-stone to "new endeavors." This becomes a recurring rhetorical move through 2019-2020 scholarship essays and shows up still-active in the 2024 6 month adventure manifesto.
  • The implicit "you" is the underrated part — at 16 he's already drafting prose that flatters a sponsor's sense of being a contribution to a longer journey. Same instinct shows up at scale in the 2026 Rankett sales scripts.

Key quote

"This is the path I plan to pursue, and with the experience I gain with you, I plan to take this path to new endeavors."

Why it matters in the graph

  • The earliest application-voice artifact in the corpus — distinct from the letter to self form (private), the manifesto style note form (broadcast), and the todo-dump form (logistic). This is voice-for-funder — a fourth Yahir register that gets sharpened across 2019-2021 scholarship essays.
  • The "new endeavors" close is structurally the same as the 2024-2025 "chapter" framing in end of chapter reflection — life-as-traversal, each stage a substrate for the next. Same rhetoric, six years apart.

Open questions

  • Which application was this for? FBLA state? Ford? An earlier scholarship? Worth cross-referencing with any April 2019 Drive docs.
  • Did the line make it into a final essay, or get cut?

Wikilinks
